chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي يَفُوتُ فِيهِ الْمُتْعَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 149 – The time in which the Tumatto is lost

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/89)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/89)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ لَا بَأْسَ لِلْمُتَمَتِّعِ إِنْ لَمْ يُحْرِمْ مِنْ لَيْلَةِ التَّرْوِيَةِ مَتَى مَا تَيَسَّرَ لَهُ مَا لَمْ يَخَفْ فَوْتَ الْمَوْقِفَيْنِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ismail Bin Marrar, from Yunus, from Yaqoub Bin Shuayb Al Maysami who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullahasws saying: ‘There is no problem for the performer of Tumatto if he does not wear Ihraam from the night of Tarwiyya (8th Zil Hijjaj) whenever it is easy for him for as long as he does not fear losing the two pausing stations’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘Isma‘il ibn Marrar from Yunus from Ya’qub ibn Shu’ayb al-Mithamiy who has said the following: “I heard abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘It is not harmful to one who is for al-Hajj al-Tamattu‘ (advantageous) to assume the state of Ihram in the night of al-Tarwiyah (eight of Dhu al-Hajj ah) whenever possible for him, as long as he is not afraid of losing the two al-Mawqafan (stations, Mina’ and ‘Arafat).”’
